summary: Iteration 24 — End-to-end testing of new HR Module under Super Admin. Backend: 23/23 pytest passing on /api/v3/hr/* — GET /dashboard (kpis active_employees/total_users/present_today/late_today/pending_leaves/monthly_salary_budget/departments + department_strength list), GET /meta (8 departments Sales/Operations/Purchase/HR/Architecture/Accounts/Planning/Quality + 7 roles), POST /employees auto-generates EMP0001-style code & defaults status=active, GET /employees with status=active/left filter, PATCH /employees/{id} partial update + 404 on bad id, DELETE /employees/{id} removes employee AND unlinks any users (verified employee_id becomes null on linked users), POST /users bcrypt-hashes password & sanitises response (no password field), employee_id=null accepted, duplicate email returns 409, GET /users enriches each user with linked_employee {employee_code, designation, full_name} + search + role filter, PATCH /users/{id}/role validates against DEFAULT_ROLES (rejects 'ceo' with 400), PATCH /users/{id}/reset-password rejects <6 chars (400) and accepts longer password, DELETE /users/{id} soft-deactivates (is_active=false), GET /branch-admin-candidates lists active branch_admin users + assigned_branch name. End-to-end flow validated: created TEST employee → created branch_admin user linked to that employee → user appears in branch-admin-candidates → user logged in successfully via /api/v3/auth/login with new bcrypt password. Frontend: super_admin login renders top-nav with 5 tabs (Master View / Marketing Board / Pre-Sales CRM / Pipeline Stages / HR Admin — all have data-testid). HR Admin renders 3 sub-tabs (Dashboard / Employees / Roles & Credentials). HR Dashboard: 5 KPI cards + Monthly Salary Budget + Departments + Department Strength grid all render without errors. Employees tab: Active/Left toggles, search input, Add Employee dialog opens with exactly 5 sub-tabs (Personal/Employment/IDs/Address/Salary). Submitted Add Employee with only full_name='TEST_UI_Emp_*' — record created and visible in table. Roles & Credentials tab: search/role filter/Create User button visible; Create User modal opens with Link to Employee (optional) dropdown, Name, Username (Email), Role, Password, Confirm Password fields. Regression: switching back to Master/Marketing/Pre-Sales/Pipeline Stages tabs not fully exercised in last run because Escape did not dismiss the Create User modal (test script issue, not product) — earlier portion already navigated all 5 tabs successfully (verified at start of run).

issues: Pre-existing console hydration warning: <span> cannot be a child of <option>. Not introduced by this iteration — same warning carried from iterations 21/22/23. Not in HR module.

action_items: (Carried over from iter 21/22/23) Remove the <span> wrapper inside <option> in CRMPage Branch Booking lead <select> to eliminate the hydration warning.
Optional: Add a visible close path (Cancel button or X icon) handler test, and ensure Esc-to-close works on the HR Create User modal (currently Esc did not dismiss it in automation — minor).
critical_code_review_comments: v3_hr.py uses _next_emp_code() based on count_documents({}) — if any employee is deleted, the next created could collide with an existing code (e.g., create 3 → delete 2 → next = EMP0003, dup). Consider a monotonically increasing counter (counters collection) or use max(employee_code) + 1.

POST /api/v3/hr/users does not validate email format or password minimum length (only PATCH reset-password enforces min 6). Creation accepts e.g. password='passpass' (ok), but also accepts password='1' if anyone sends it. Add a min_length=6 to UserAccountCreate.password.

GET /branch-admin-candidates does NOT respect any filter for soft-deactivated users — query has is_active:true so that's fine, but assigned_branch lookup uses {b.get('admin_user_id'): b} which silently keys None for branches with no admin. Could collide if multiple un-assigned branches exist. Use a dict keyed only when admin_user_id is set.

DELETE /employees/{id} hard-deletes from DB. For audit/HR compliance you may prefer a soft-delete (status='deleted') + retain employee_code history. Currently leaves orphan users (employee_id set to null) with no audit trail.
v3_hr.py has no rate-limiting / lockout on /reset-password — super_admin can reset to any value. Acceptable since only super_admin can call it, but log changes for audit.
All HR write endpoints correctly require super_admin via v3_require_roles; read endpoints correctly also allow marketing_head — confirmed via tests.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% — full nav, all 3 HR sub-tabs, dashboard, employee create, roles tab, create-user modal verified; only the modal-dismiss + post-modal regression hop wasn't completed due to test-script Esc handling (product not affected; product modal renders correctly with all required fields and Cancel/Submit buttons).

test_credentials: From /app/memory/test_credentials.md — super_admin admin@fitsiomax.com/admin123. Test created branch_admin users (TEST_BranchAdmin / test_user_*@fitsiomax.com / branchpass1→newpass1234) which are cleaned up via test teardown.
seed_data_creation: Test created and cleaned up TEST_Emp_*, TEST_BranchAdmin, TEST_Unlinked employees/users via fixtures. One TEST_UI_Emp_* employee (from frontend test) and the TEST_BranchAdmin / TEST_Unlinked / TEST_PreSales_* / TEST_JrPhysio_* etc rows visible in the Roles screenshot may persist if backend cleanup loop wasn't fully reached — harmless leftovers.
context_for_next_testing_agent: HR module is fully wired end-to-end. All /api/v3/hr/* routes verified. Super-admin only for writes; super_admin + marketing_head for reads. HR Board lives at /app/frontend/src/components/hr/HRBoard.jsx (data-testids: hr-board, hr-subtab-{dashboard|employees|roles}, hr-kpi-*, hr-emp-* for employees flow, hr-roles-* + hr-create-user-modal for users flow). EMP code is auto-generated as EMP0001-style — be aware of the potential duplicate bug after deletes (see code review). PATCH role/reset-password use query params (?role= / ?password=), not JSON body. Employee delete unlinks users (sets employee_id=null).
rca_of_the_issue: Only one test self-corrected during run: test_meta_requires_auth initially asserted 401/403 but FastAPI returns 422 when the Authorization header is missing (Pydantic dependency validation fires before role check). Test was adjusted to accept 401/403/422. No product bug. All other 22 tests passed first try.
